content.body: Statistics involves collecting, organising, and interpreting data. Measures of central tendency describe the 'typical' value in a data set. Measures of spread describe how spread out the data is.

MEAN (Average):
Mean = Sum of all values ÷ Number of values

MEDIAN (Middle value):
Arrange data in order, find the middle value.
If even number of values, median = average of the two middle values.

MODE:
The value that appears most often.
A data set can have no mode, one mode, or multiple modes.

RANGE (Spread):
Range = Highest value − Lowest value

content.title: Finding Averages
content.body:
Data: 3, 7, 5, 9, 5, 8, 4, 6, 5

Mean = (3+7+5+9+5+8+4+6+5)/9 = 52/9 = 5.78

Ordered: 3, 4, 5, 5, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9
Median = 5 (5th value in 9 values)

Mode = 5 (appears 3 times)

Range = 9 − 3 = 6

content.title: Even Number of Values
content.body:
Data: 12, 15, 8, 20, 14, 18
Ordered: 8, 12, 14, 15, 18, 20
Median = (14+15)/2 = 14.5 (average of 3rd and 4th)
Mean = (8+12+14+15+18+20)/6 = 87/6 = 14.5
Mode = none (all values appear once)
Range = 20 − 8 = 12

When to use each average:
Mean: best when data is symmetric with no extreme outliers
Median: best when data has outliers (e.g. income data where a few very rich people skew the mean)
Mode: best for categorical data (e.g. most popular shoe size)

A very high or low value (outlier) greatly affects the mean but NOT the median.

questions.question: Find the median of: 3, 1, 7, 9, 5
questions.explanation: Ordered: 1, 3, 5, 7, 9. Middle (3rd) value = 5.

questions.question: Find the mode of: 2, 5, 3, 2, 8, 5, 2
questions.explanation: 2 appears 3 times (most frequent). Mode = 2.

questions.question: Find the range of: 15, 3, 22, 8, 17
questions.explanation: Range = highest − lowest = 22 − 3 = 19.

questions.question: Ages: 12, 13, 12, 14, 12, 15. Find the mode.
questions.explanation: 12 appears 3 times — more than any other value. Mode = 12.

questions.question: Why is median preferred over mean for income data?
options: Median is not affected by extreme outliers
questions.answer: Median is not affected by extreme outliers
questions.explanation: A few very high earners raise the mean significantly, making it misleading. The median better represents the 'typical' income.

questions.question: Data set has no repeated values. Its mode is:
questions.explanation: If no value appears more than once, the data set has no mode.

questions.question: Mean of 5 numbers is 8. What is their sum?
questions.explanation: Mean = Sum/n → Sum = Mean × n = 8 × 5 = 40.

questions.question: What does simplifying an expression mean?
options: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.answer: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.explanation: Simplification means rewriting an expression in its simplest equivalent form by collecting like terms and applying arithmetic.

questions.question: Which of these is an example of a variable?
questions.explanation: A variable is a letter (like x, y, n) that represents an unknown or changing value.

questions.question: What is 2x + 3x simplified?
questions.explanation: Collect like terms: 2x + 3x = 5x (both terms have the same variable x).

questions.question: Solve for x: x + 7 = 15
questions.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides: x = 15 - 7 = 8.

questions.question: What is the result of expanding 4(y - 3)?
questions.explanation: Distribute: 4 × y - 4 × 3 = 4y - 12.

questions.question: Which operation reverses addition?
questions.explanation: Subtraction is the inverse (reverse) of addition.

questions.question: What is 3² equal to?
questions.explanation: 3² = 3 × 3 = 9. The exponent 2 means multiply the base by itself.

questions.question: Factorise: 4x + 8
questions.explanation: The HCF of 4x and 8 is 4. So 4x + 8 = 4(x + 2).

questions.question: What is the y-intercept of y = 3x + 5?
questions.explanation: In y = mx + c, c is the y-intercept. Here c = 5.

questions.question: Solve: 3x = 18
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 3: x = 18 ÷ 3 = 6.

content.body: When dealing with large data sets, we organise data into frequency tables. This makes it easier to find averages and spot patterns. Histograms and bar charts let us visualise data.

Frequency Table Terms:
Frequency (f): how many times a value occurs
Relative frequency: f/total
Cumulative frequency: running total of frequencies

Mean from frequency table:
Mean = Σ(x × f) / Σf
(multiply each value by its frequency, sum up, divide by total frequency)

content.title: Mean from Frequency Table
content.body:
Number of goals scored per match:
Goals(x): 0  1  2  3  4
Freq(f):   3  5  6  4  2

Σf = 3+5+6+4+2 = 20 matches
Σ(xf) = (0×3)+(1×5)+(2×6)+(3×4)+(4×2)
     = 0 + 5 + 12 + 12 + 8 = 37
Mean = 37/20 = 1.85 goals per match

content.title: Bar Chart vs Histogram
content.body:
Bar chart: used for DISCRETE or CATEGORICAL data
• Bars have gaps between them
• Height = frequency

Histogram: used for CONTINUOUS data in class intervals
• NO gaps between bars
• Area (not height) represents frequency
• Frequency density = frequency / class width

questions.question: In a frequency table, what does 'f' represent?
questions.explanation: Frequency (f) is the count of how many times each value appears in the data.

questions.question: x: 1,2,3; f: 4,6,2. Find Σf.
questions.explanation: Σf = 4+6+2 = 12 (total number of values).

questions.question: A histogram differs from a bar chart because:
options: There are no gaps between bars and it shows continuous data
It uses lines instead of bars
questions.answer: There are no gaps between bars and it shows continuous data
questions.explanation: Histograms show continuous data with no gaps — the area of each bar represents frequency.

questions.question: Frequency density = ?
questions.explanation: Frequency density = frequency ÷ class width. Used in histograms when class widths differ.

questions.question: Cumulative frequency is:
options: Running total of all frequencies up to that point
questions.answer: Running total of all frequencies up to that point
questions.explanation: Cumulative frequency adds up all frequencies from the beginning to each point.

questions.question: f: 5, 8, 7, 3, 2. Total = 25. Relative frequency of class with f=8:
questions.explanation: Relative frequency = f/total = 8/25 = 0.32.

questions.question: In a bar chart for shoe sizes, gaps between bars indicate:
options: Discrete data (shoe sizes are separate values)
questions.answer: Discrete data (shoe sizes are separate values)
questions.explanation: Bar charts with gaps are used for discrete (countable, separate) data like shoe sizes.

questions.question: 50 students' test scores. Modal class is 60-70 with f=18. What fraction scored in this range?
questions.explanation: Relative frequency = 18/50 = 0.36 (or 36%).

questions.question: What does simplifying an expression mean?
options: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.answer: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.explanation: Simplification means rewriting an expression in its simplest equivalent form by collecting like terms and applying arithmetic.

questions.explanation: A variable is a letter (like x, y, n) that represents an unknown or changing value.

questions.explanation: Collect like terms: 2x + 3x = 5x (both terms have the same variable x).

questions.question: Solve for x: x + 7 = 15
questions.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides: x = 15 - 7 = 8.

questions.question: What is the result of expanding 4(y - 3)?
questions.explanation: Distribute: 4 × y - 4 × 3 = 4y - 12.

questions.question: Which operation reverses addition?
questions.explanation: Subtraction is the inverse (reverse) of addition.

questions.explanation: 3² = 3 × 3 = 9. The exponent 2 means multiply the base by itself.

questions.question: Factorise: 4x + 8
questions.explanation: The HCF of 4x and 8 is 4. So 4x + 8 = 4(x + 2).

questions.question: What is the y-intercept of y = 3x + 5?
questions.explanation: In y = mx + c, c is the y-intercept. Here c = 5.

questions.question: Solve: 3x = 18
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 3: x = 18 ÷ 3 = 6.

content.body:
P(event) = Number of favourable outcomes / Total number of outcomes
0 ≤ P(A) ≤ 1
P(certain event) = 1, P(impossible event) = 0
P(A') = 1 − P(A) (complementary probability)

content.title: Basic Probability
content.body:
A bag has 3 red, 4 blue, 5 green balls (12 total)
P(red) = 3/12 = 1/4
P(not green) = 7/12
P(blue or green) = 9/12 = 3/4

Addition Rule:
P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B) − P(A and B)
If A and B are mutually exclusive: P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B)

Multiplication Rule:
P(A and B) = P(A) × P(B|A)
If A and B are independent: P(A and B) = P(A) × P(B)

questions.question: A die is rolled. P(getting 4) =
questions.explanation: A standard die has 6 faces. Only one face shows 4. P(4) = 1/6

questions.question: A card is drawn from 52 cards. P(heart) =
questions.explanation: There are 13 hearts in 52 cards. P(heart) = 13/52 = 1/4

questions.question: Two coins are tossed. P(both heads) =
questions.explanation: P(H)×P(H) = 1/2 × 1/2 = 1/4 (independent events)

questions.question: Events A and B are mutually exclusive if...
questions.explanation: Mutually exclusive events cannot occur simultaneously: P(A and B)=0

questions.question: P(A)=0.4, P(B)=0.3, P(A and B)=0.1. Find P(A or B).
A bag has 2 red and 3 blue balls. Two drawn without replacement. P(both red) =
What probability value means impossible?
questions.explanation: An impossible event has probability 0. Probability ranges from 0 to 1.

questions.question: P(A)=0.6, P(B)=0.5, independent. P(A and B)=
questions.explanation: For independent events: P(A and B)=P(A)×P(B)=0.6×0.5=0.3

questions.question: In an experiment, the sum of all probabilities of all outcomes equals...
questions.explanation: The probabilities of all possible outcomes must sum to 1 (certainty).

questions.question: What does simplifying an expression mean?
options: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.answer: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.explanation: Simplification means rewriting an expression in its simplest equivalent form by collecting like terms and applying arithmetic.

questions.explanation: A variable is a letter (like x, y, n) that represents an unknown or changing value.

questions.question: What is 2x + 3x simplified?
questions.explanation: Collect like terms: 2x + 3x = 5x (both terms have the same variable x).

questions.question: Solve for x: x + 7 = 15
questions.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides: x = 15 - 7 = 8.

questions.question: What is the result of expanding 4(y - 3)?
questions.explanation: Distribute: 4 × y - 4 × 3 = 4y - 12.

questions.question: Which operation reverses addition?
questions.explanation: Subtraction is the inverse (reverse) of addition.

questions.question: What is 3² equal to?
questions.explanation: 3² = 3 × 3 = 9. The exponent 2 means multiply the base by itself.

questions.question: Factorise: 4x + 8
questions.explanation: The HCF of 4x and 8 is 4. So 4x + 8 = 4(x + 2).

questions.question: What is the y-intercept of y = 3x + 5?
questions.explanation: In y = mx + c, c is the y-intercept. Here c = 5.

questions.question: Solve: 3x = 18
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 3: x = 18 ÷ 3 = 6.

content.body:
Range = Maximum value − Minimum value

Variance = Σ(x − x̄)²/n
Standard Deviation = √Variance

Interquartile Range (IQR) = Q3 − Q1
Q1 = lower quartile (25th percentile)
Q3 = upper quartile (75th percentile)

content.title: Standard Deviation Example
content.body:
Data: 3, 5, 7, 5, 10
Mean x̄ = 30/5 = 6
Deviations: −3, −1, 1, −1, 4
Squared deviations: 9, 1, 1, 1, 16
Variance = 28/5 = 5.6
SD = √5.6 ≈ 2.37
A small standard deviation means data is clustered close to the mean. A large standard deviation means data is spread out. Two datasets can have the same mean but very different standard deviations.

questions.question: What does a large standard deviation indicate?
questions.explanation: Standard deviation measures spread. Large SD means values are widely spread from the mean.

questions.question: Data: 2,4,6,8,10. What is the range?
questions.explanation: Range = max−min = 10−2 = 8

questions.question: What is the IQR?
questions.explanation: IQR (Interquartile Range) = Q3 − Q1, the range of the middle 50% of data.

questions.question: Data: 5,5,5,5,5. What is the standard deviation?
questions.explanation: When all values are equal, every deviation from the mean is 0. Variance and SD are both 0.

questions.question: Variance of dataset X is 9. What is the standard deviation?
questions.explanation: Standard deviation = √variance = √9 = 3

questions.question: Which measure is NOT affected by outliers?
questions.explanation: IQR only uses the middle 50% of data (Q1 to Q3), so extreme values (outliers) don't affect it.

questions.question: Data: 1,2,3,4,5. Mean=3. Variance=?
questions.explanation: Deviations: −2,−1,0,1,2. Squared: 4,1,0,1,4. Sum=10. Variance=10/5=2

questions.question: Why is standard deviation preferred over variance?
options: It has the same units as the data
questions.answer: It has the same units as the data
questions.explanation: Variance uses squared units (e.g. cm²). Standard deviation is the square root, restoring original units (cm).

questions.question: In a box plot, what does the box represent?
questions.explanation: The box in a box-and-whisker plot spans from Q1 to Q3, representing the IQR (middle 50% of data).

questions.question: Two classes have the same mean test score. Class A has SD=2, Class B has SD=10. What can you conclude?
options: Class A scores are more consistent
Class B scores are more consistent
questions.answer: Class A scores are more consistent
questions.explanation: Lower SD means scores are closer to the mean — more consistent. Class A is more consistent.

options: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.answer: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.explanation: Simplification means rewriting an expression in its simplest equivalent form by collecting like terms and applying arithmetic.

content.body: In this lesson, we explore advanced aspects of Statistics Basics. Build on what you have already learned and apply your knowledge to more challenging problems.
Learning Objectives

By the end of this lesson you should be able to: (1) Apply Statistics Basics concepts to complex problems. (2) Use appropriate formulas and methods. (3) Interpret results in context. (4) Connect this topic to related areas of mathematics.
Core Theory

This section of Statistics Basics builds on foundational knowledge. In mathematics, advanced problem-solving requires not just knowing formulas but understanding when and how to apply them. Study each worked example carefully.

content.title: Worked Example 5.1
content.body: Consider a more complex problem on Statistics Basics. Approach: (1) Read and understand the problem. (2) Identify all given information. (3) Determine which concepts apply. (4) Solve systematically, showing all steps. (5) Check your answer is reasonable.

content.title: Worked Example 5.2
content.body: Another type of problem you may encounter: Questions may test your ability to combine multiple concepts within Statistics Basics. Practice identifying the type of problem and the appropriate method before attempting to solve.

Common examination questions on Statistics Basics include: definitions and explanations, numerical calculations, diagram interpretation, and analysis of given data. Make sure you can handle all types.
Summary and Review

You have now covered all lessons in Statistics Basics. Review the key concepts from each lesson, practice past paper questions, and ensure you can apply your knowledge confidently. Good luck in your studies!

questions.question: What does simplifying an expression mean?
options: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.answer: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.explanation: Simplification means rewriting an expression in its simplest equivalent form by collecting like terms and applying arithmetic.

questions.question: Which of these is an example of a variable?
questions.explanation: A variable is a letter (like x, y, n) that represents an unknown or changing value.

questions.question: What is 2x + 3x simplified?
questions.explanation: Collect like terms: 2x + 3x = 5x (both terms have the same variable x).

questions.question: Solve for x: x + 7 = 15
questions.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides: x = 15 - 7 = 8.

questions.question: What is the result of expanding 4(y - 3)?
questions.explanation: Distribute: 4 × y - 4 × 3 = 4y - 12.

questions.question: Which operation reverses addition?
questions.explanation: Subtraction is the inverse (reverse) of addition.

questions.question: What is 3² equal to?
questions.explanation: 3² = 3 × 3 = 9. The exponent 2 means multiply the base by itself.

questions.question: Factorise: 4x + 8
questions.explanation: The HCF of 4x and 8 is 4. So 4x + 8 = 4(x + 2).

questions.question: What is the y-intercept of y = 3x + 5?
questions.explanation: In y = mx + c, c is the y-intercept. Here c = 5.

questions.question: Solve: 3x = 18
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 3: x = 18 ÷ 3 = 6.

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a rectangle?
questions.explanation: Area of a rectangle = length × width.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a like term with 3x²?
questions.explanation: Like terms must have the same variable AND the same power. 3x has power 1, not 2, so it is not a like term with 3x².

questions.question: What is the gradient/slope of a horizontal line?
questions.explanation: A horizontal line has no rise, so gradient = rise/run = 0/run = 0.

questions.question: If a = 4 and b = 3, find 2a + b.
questions.explanation: Substitute: 2(4) + 3 = 8 + 3 = 11.

questions.question: What does the inequality x > 5 mean?
questions.explanation: The > symbol means 'greater than'. x > 5 means x takes values greater than (but not equal to) 5.

questions.question: Solve: 2(x + 3) = 14
questions.explanation: Expand: 2x + 6 = 14. Subtract 6: 2x = 8. Divide: x = 4.

questions.question: The HCF of 12 and 18 is:
questions.explanation: Factors of 12: 1,2,3,4,6,12. Factors of 18: 1,2,3,6,9,18. Highest common factor = 6.

questions.question: What is the LCM of 4 and 6?
questions.explanation: Multiples of 4: 4,8,12,... Multiples of 6: 6,12,... Lowest common multiple = 12.

questions.question: Which number is prime?
questions.explanation: A prime number has exactly two factors: 1 and itself. 7 is prime (factors: 1 and 7 only).
